# Multi-Location Management PRP - BasicAgInventory
<!-- Generated by CODEX Enhanced PRP Creation - Complete Workflow Context Integration -->

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Enable farmers to organize inventory across multiple farm locations (barns, fields, storage facilities) with GPS coordinates, visual mapping, and seamless item transfers between locations, providing real-time visibility of inventory distribution across the entire farm operation.

**Deliverable**: SwiftUI-based location management system with Core Location integration, implementing location-aware inventory tracking, transfer workflows, and GPS coordinate capture following MVVM architecture patterns.

**Success Definition**:
- Users can create and manage farm locations with GPS coordinates in under 45 seconds
- Item transfers between locations complete with full audit trail and location history
- Location-based inventory views load within 1 second for any farm location
- GPS coordinate capture works reliably in outdoor farm environments

## User Persona

**Target User**: Farm Manager Sarah - Owner/operator managing inventory across multiple farm buildings and field locations

**Use Case**: Daily inventory tracking across distributed farm locations, moving equipment and supplies between barns, fields, and storage facilities during seasonal operations

**User Journey**:
1. Create farm locations with GPS coordinates and descriptions
2. Assign new inventory items to specific locations during intake
3. View inventory distribution across all farm locations
4. Transfer items between locations with quantity tracking
5. Generate location-specific reports for operational planning
6. Track location history for compliance and audit purposes

**Pain Points Addressed**:
- Difficulty tracking where items are stored across large farm operations
- Manual tracking of item movements between locations
- Lack of visibility into inventory distribution
- Time-consuming location-based inventory counts

## Why

**Business Value**:
- Reduces time spent searching for equipment across farm locations by 50%
- Enables efficient inventory redistribution based on seasonal needs
- Supports compliance reporting with location-based audit trails
- Improves operational efficiency through location-aware inventory planning

**Technical Requirements**:
- FR7: Create and manage multiple farm locations with GPS coordinates
- FR8: Move inventory between locations with transaction tracking
- FR9: View inventory distributed across all locations on unified dashboard
- FR10: Generate location-specific inventory reports

**Architecture Decisions**:
- Core Location Services integration for GPS coordinate capture
- Location entity relationships with inventory items in Core Data
- MapKit integration for visual location representation
- Transaction logging for complete transfer audit trail

**Integration Needs**:
- Core Location Services for GPS coordinate capture and accuracy
- MapKit for location visualization and farm layout mapping
- Core Data relationships between Location and InventoryItem entities
- Background location updates for field-based operations

## What

This implementation creates a comprehensive location management system that integrates GPS coordinates, visual mapping, and inventory distribution tracking. The system enables farmers to organize their operations spatially, providing clear visibility into where items are stored and facilitating efficient transfers between locations.

**Core Features**:
- **Location Creation**: Add farm locations with names, descriptions, and GPS coordinates
- **GPS Integration**: Automatic coordinate capture using device location services
- **Location Mapping**: Visual representation of farm locations using MapKit
- **Inventory Assignment**: Associate inventory items with specific farm locations
- **Transfer Management**: Move items between locations with quantity splitting
- **Distribution Dashboard**: Overview of inventory spread across all locations
- **Location Reports**: Generate location-specific inventory summaries
- **Transfer History**: Complete audit trail of all item movements

### Success Criteria

- [ ] Create new farm location with GPS coordinates in under 45 seconds
- [ ] GPS coordinate capture accuracy within 10 meters in outdoor farm environments
- [ ] Item transfer between locations completes with full transaction logging
- [ ] Location-based inventory views load within 1 second
- [ ] Support for unlimited farm locations with maintained performance
- [ ] Transfer history provides complete audit trail for compliance
- [ ] Visual map representation works on iPhone and iPad devices
- [ ] Offline location management with GPS coordinates stored locally

### Known Gotchas & Constraints

```swift
// CRITICAL: Core Location and MapKit integration constraints

// Core Location Permission Requirements:
// - Must request permission before accessing location services
// - Handle permission denial gracefully with manual coordinate entry
// - Location accuracy may vary in rural areas with poor GPS signal
import CoreLocation

class LocationManager: NSObject, CLLocationManagerDelegate, ObservableObject {
    private let manager = CLLocationManager()

    override init() {
        super.init()
        manager.delegate = self
        // Configure for farm environment - best accuracy for outdoor use
        manager.desiredAccuracy = kCLLocationAccuracyBest
    }

    func requestLocationPermission() {
        manager.requestWhenInUseAuthorization()
    }
}

// MapKit SwiftUI Integration Constraints:
// - MKMapView requires UIViewRepresentable wrapper for SwiftUI
// - Map annotations must implement MKAnnotation protocol
// - Coordinate updates must happen on main thread for UI responsiveness

// Core Data Relationship Constraints:
// - Location to InventoryItem: One-to-Many with cascade delete rules
// - Transfer audit trail requires separate Transaction entity
// - GPS coordinates stored as Double with validation for valid ranges

// Performance Constraints from Architecture:
// - Location views must load within 1 second (NFR2 applied to locations)
// - GPS coordinate capture timeout of 30 seconds for farm environments
// - Map rendering optimized for rural areas with limited data connectivity

// Privacy and Security Requirements:
// - All location data stored locally only (no cloud transmission)
// - User consent required before accessing device location
// - GPS coordinates encrypted at rest via Core Data encryption
```

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data Models and Structure

```swift
// Location entity for farm location management
// Extends existing Core Data model with GPS and relationship support

import CoreData
import CoreLocation
import Foundation

@objc(Location)
public class Location: NSManagedObject {
    @NSManaged public var id: UUID
    @NSManaged public var name: String
    @NSManaged public var locationDescription: String
    @NSManaged public var latitude: Double
    @NSManaged public var longitude: Double
    @NSManaged public var createdAt: Date
    @NSManaged public var updatedAt: Date

    // Relationships with inventory management
    @NSManaged public var inventoryItems: NSSet?
    @NSManaged public var incomingTransfers: NSSet?
    @NSManaged public var outgoingTransfers: NSSet?

    // Computed properties for GPS integration
    var coordinate: CLLocationCoordinate2D {
        CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude)
    }

    var clLocation: CLLocation {
        CLLocation(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude)
    }

    // Inventory count for dashboard display
    var inventoryItemCount: Int {
        inventoryItems?.count ?? 0
    }

    // Validation for GPS coordinates
    func validate() throws {
        guard !name.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty else {
            throw LocationError.invalidName
        }
        guard CLLocationCoordinate2DIsValid(coordinate) else {
            throw LocationError.invalidCoordinates
        }
    }
}

// Transfer transaction entity for audit trail
@objc(Transfer)
public class Transfer: NSManagedObject {
    @NSManaged public var id: UUID
    @NSManaged public var quantity: Int32
    @NSManaged public var transferDate: Date
    @NSManaged public var notes: String?
    @NSManaged public var status: String // "pending", "completed", "cancelled"

    // Relationships
    @NSManaged public var inventoryItem: InventoryItem
    @NSManaged public var fromLocation: Location?
    @NSManaged public var toLocation: Location
    @NSManaged public var initiatedBy: String // User identifier

    // Business logic for transfer validation
    var canComplete: Bool {
        guard let fromLocation = fromLocation else { return true } // New item assignment
        return inventoryItem.quantity >= quantity
    }
}

// MapKit annotation for farm locations
import MapKit

class LocationAnnotation: NSObject, MKAnnotation {
    let location: Location

    init(location: Location) {
        self.location = location
        super.init()
    }

    var coordinate: CLLocationCoordinate2D {
        location.coordinate
    }

    var title: String? {
        location.name
    }

    var subtitle: String? {
        "\(location.inventoryItemCount) items"
    }
}

// Domain-specific error handling
enum LocationError: Error, LocalizedError {
    case invalidName
    case invalidCoordinates
    case gpsUnavailable
    case transferFailed
    case insufficientQuantity

    var errorDescription: String? {
        switch self {
        case .invalidName:
            return "Location name cannot be empty"
        case .invalidCoordinates:
            return "GPS coordinates are invalid"
        case .gpsUnavailable:
            return "GPS location unavailable. Enter coordinates manually."
        case .transferFailed:
            return "Item transfer failed. Please try again."
        case .insufficientQuantity:
            return "Not enough items available at source location"
        }
    }
}
```

### Implementation Patterns & Key Details

```swift
// Location Service Pattern with Core Location integration
import CoreLocation
import Combine

class LocationService: NSObject, ObservableObject, CLLocationManagerDelegate {
    @Published var currentLocation: CLLocation?
    @Published var authorizationStatus: CLAuthorizationStatus = .notDetermined
    @Published var locationError: Error?

    private let locationManager = CLLocationManager()
    private var locationCompletionHandler: ((Result<CLLocation, Error>) -> Void)?

    override init() {
        super.init()
        locationManager.delegate = self
        locationManager.desiredAccuracy = kCLLocationAccuracyBest
        locationManager.distanceFilter = 10 // meters
    }

    func requestLocationPermission() {
        locationManager.requestWhenInUseAuthorization()
    }

    // Capture GPS coordinates for new farm location
    func captureCurrentLocation() async throws -> CLLocation {
        return try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation { continuation in
            locationCompletionHandler = continuation.resume

            guard authorizationStatus == .authorizedWhenInUse || authorizationStatus == .authorizedAlways else {
                continuation.resume(throwing: LocationError.gpsUnavailable)
                return
            }

            locationManager.requestLocation()

            // Timeout for rural GPS challenges
            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 30) {
                self.locationCompletionHandler = nil
                continuation.resume(throwing: LocationError.gpsUnavailable)
            }
        }
    }

    // CLLocationManagerDelegate implementation
    func locationManager(_ manager: CLLocationManager, didUpdateLocations locations: [CLLocation]) {
        guard let location = locations.last else { return }
        currentLocation = location
        locationCompletionHandler?(.success(location))
        locationCompletionHandler = nil
    }

    func locationManager(_ manager: CLLocationManager, didFailWithError error: Error) {
        locationError = error
        locationCompletionHandler?(.failure(error))
        locationCompletionHandler = nil
    }
}

// Repository Pattern for Location Data Operations
protocol LocationRepositoryProtocol {
    func fetchAllLocations() async throws -> [Location]
    func createLocation(name: String, description: String, coordinate: CLLocationCoordinate2D) async throws -> Location
    func updateLocation(_ location: Location) async throws
    func deleteLocation(_ location: Location) async throws
    func fetchLocation(by id: UUID) async throws -> Location?
    func fetchInventoryItems(for location: Location) async throws -> [InventoryItem]
}

class CoreDataLocationRepository: LocationRepositoryProtocol {
    private let context: NSManagedObjectContext

    init(context: NSManagedObjectContext) {
        self.context = context
    }

    func createLocation(name: String, description: String, coordinate: CLLocationCoordinate2D) async throws -> Location {
        let location = Location(context: context)
        location.id = UUID()
        location.name = name
        location.locationDescription = description
        location.latitude = coordinate.latitude
        location.longitude = coordinate.longitude
        location.createdAt = Date()
        location.updatedAt = Date()

        try location.validate()

        try await context.perform {
            try self.context.save()
        }

        return location
    }
}

// MapKit SwiftUI Integration Pattern
import SwiftUI
import MapKit

struct LocationMapView: UIViewRepresentable {
    @Binding var locations: [Location]
    @Binding var selectedLocation: Location?

    func makeUIView(context: Context) -> MKMapView {
        let mapView = MKMapView()
        mapView.delegate = context.coordinator
        mapView.showsUserLocation = true
        mapView.userTrackingMode = .none
        return mapView
    }

    func updateUIView(_ mapView: MKMapView, context: Context) {
        let annotations = locations.map { LocationAnnotation(location: $0) }

        mapView.removeAnnotations(mapView.annotations)
        mapView.addAnnotations(annotations)

        if !locations.isEmpty {
            let region = calculateMapRegion(for: locations)
            mapView.setRegion(region, animated: true)
        }
    }

    func makeCoordinator() -> Coordinator {
        Coordinator(self)
    }

    class Coordinator: NSObject, MKMapViewDelegate {
        var parent: LocationMapView

        init(_ parent: LocationMapView) {
            self.parent = parent
        }

        func mapView(_ mapView: MKMapView, didSelect view: MKAnnotationView) {
            if let annotation = view.annotation as? LocationAnnotation {
                parent.selectedLocation = annotation.location
            }
        }
    }

    private func calculateMapRegion(for locations: [Location]) -> MKCoordinateRegion {
        let coordinates = locations.map { $0.coordinate }
        let minLat = coordinates.map { $0.latitude }.min() ?? 0
        let maxLat = coordinates.map { $0.latitude }.max() ?? 0
        let minLon = coordinates.map { $0.longitude }.min() ?? 0
        let maxLon = coordinates.map { $0.longitude }.max() ?? 0

        let center = CLLocationCoordinate2D(
            latitude: (minLat + maxLat) / 2,
            longitude: (minLon + maxLon) / 2
        )

        let span = MKCoordinateSpan(
            latitudeDelta: (maxLat - minLat) * 1.2,
            longitudeDelta: (maxLon - minLon) * 1.2
        )

        return MKCoordinateRegion(center: center, span: span)
    }
}

// Transfer ViewModel Pattern
@MainActor
class ItemTransferViewModel: ObservableObject {
    @Published var sourceLocation: Location?
    @Published var destinationLocation: Location?
    @Published var selectedItem: InventoryItem?
    @Published var transferQuantity: Int32 = 1
    @Published var notes: String = ""
    @Published var isTransferring: Bool = false
    @Published var transferError: Error?

    private let transferService: TransferService
    private let locationRepository: LocationRepositoryProtocol

    init(transferService: TransferService, locationRepository: LocationRepositoryProtocol) {
        self.transferService = transferService
        self.locationRepository = locationRepository
    }

    var canTransfer: Bool {
        guard let item = selectedItem,
              let destination = destinationLocation,
              transferQuantity > 0 else { return false }

        // Check quantity availability
        if let source = sourceLocation {
            return item.quantity >= transferQuantity
        }

        return true // New item assignment
    }

    func executeTransfer() async {
        guard canTransfer,
              let item = selectedItem,
              let destination = destinationLocation else { return }

        isTransferring = true
        transferError = nil

        do {
            try await transferService.transferItem(
                item,
                from: sourceLocation,
                to: destination,
                quantity: transferQuantity,
                notes: notes
            )

            // Reset form on success
            resetTransferForm()

        } catch {
            transferError = error
        }

        isTransferring = false
    }

    private func resetTransferForm() {
        sourceLocation = nil
        destinationLocation = nil
        selectedItem = nil
        transferQuantity = 1
        notes = ""
    }
}
```

### Integration Points

```yaml
INTERNAL_INTEGRATION:
  - component: InventoryManagement
    interface: Shared InventoryItem entities with location relationships
    data_flow: Location assignment during item creation and transfer operations
    error_handling: Consistent error handling patterns across location and inventory

  - component: CoreDataManager
    interface: Shared NSManagedObjectContext for transaction consistency
    data_flow: Location and Transfer entities persisted with inventory items
    error_handling: Database transaction rollback for failed transfers

EXTERNAL_INTEGRATION:
  - service: Core Location Services
    protocol: CLLocationManager delegate pattern for GPS coordinate capture
    authentication: User location permission with "when in use" authorization
    fallback: Manual coordinate entry when GPS unavailable

  - service: MapKit Framework
    protocol: MKMapView delegate for map interaction and annotation selection
    authentication: No authentication required for local map rendering
    fallback: List-based location management when MapKit unavailable

STATE_MANAGEMENT:
  - pattern: SwiftUI MVVM with location-aware ObservableObject ViewModels
  - persistence: Core Data relationships between Location and InventoryItem entities
  - synchronization: Real-time UI updates via Combine publishers and @Published properties
```

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- ❌ Don't access location services without user permission - always check authorization status
- ❌ Don't store GPS coordinates in UserDefaults - use encrypted Core Data only
- ❌ Don't ignore GPS accuracy limitations in rural areas - provide manual fallbacks
- ❌ Don't perform MapKit operations on background threads - use main queue for UI updates
- ❌ Don't skip transfer validation - always check quantity availability before transfer
- ❌ Don't bypass Core Data relationships - maintain referential integrity for locations
- ❌ Don't ignore battery impact - configure location services for farm environment efficiency
- ❌ Don't implement transfers without audit trails - compliance requires complete logging

## Confidence Score: 9/10

High confidence based on complete iOS Core Location and MapKit integration patterns, comprehensive transfer workflow design, and full context synthesis from architecture and requirements documents.
